claim: Hermes persistent memory allows agents to learn trading style across conversations, building skills files that improve accuracy over time without explicit fine-tuning
actionable: Use Hermes for multi-turn trading workflows; provide portfolio context once, then issue simple commands like 'scan for longs' knowing the agent retains your strategy
source_title: Build a Self Learning AI Trading Agent with Hermes (Full Setup Guide)

claim: Cron jobs in Hermes enable 24/7 automation (morning briefs, alert scanning) via messaging platform, eliminating IDE dependencies for repetitive agent tasks
actionable: Schedule agent tasks in Hermes using plain English: 'set up daily morning briefing at 8am' instead of writing scripts; cron jobs execute on VPS
source_title: Build a Self Learning AI Trading Agent with Hermes (Full Setup Guide)

claim: VPS-based agents (Hostinger KVM2 sufficient) run 24/7 without local machine overhead; distributed models benefit from always-on infrastructure with stateful connections
actionable: Deploy multi-agent systems on a $50-100/yr VPS; avoid paying for Mac minis or keeping laptops open; maintain persistent state across agent instances
source_title: Build a Self Learning AI Trading Agent with Hermes (Full Setup Guide)

claim: Thinker-talker pattern: Hermes can separate agents by role (trader vs. researcher vs. briefer) for specialized expertise; parallel agents outperform single generalist
actionable: Create separate Hermes instances for distinct roles; route tasks by domain (portfolio analysis → trader agent, news research → researcher agent)
source_title: Build a Self Learning AI Trading Agent with Hermes (Full Setup Guide)
